validation.method: Consolidated 3-reviewer DISTILL gate (Eclipse/Architect/Sentinel) approved 0 blockers. Cross-repo TS/Playwright/docs feature; real stacks Vitest (website) + Playwright @screenshot (Lighthouse), not pytest/des-Python. Roadmap self-approved by orchestrator given tiny scope + passed gate.

phases.name: Walking skeleton — one image via jsDelivr CDN (website repo)
phases.description: Prove the jsDelivr @main hotlink boundary (DDD-2/DDD-3) live with one real image before any bulk migration. Ship the lighthouseAsset() URL-convention helper (DDD-8) as the reusable abstraction first.

steps.name: lighthouseAsset() helper + Vitest + migrate one screenshot to jsDelivr CDN
steps.criteria: WEBSITE REPO at /storage/repos/website. lighthouseAsset('features/metrics/metricsoverview.png') returns 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/LetPeopleWork/Lighthouse@main/docs/assets/features/metrics/metricsoverview.png'; it normalizes a leading slash (e.g. '/features/teamdetail.png') without a double slash and without dropping segments; a nested path round-trips. ONE marketing image in LighthouseSection.tsx is changed from a bundled '@/assets/screenshots/*.png' import to lighthouseAsset(...) in mediaItems. Vitest unit spec for lighthouseAsset passes. Website build (tsc -b + vite) clean and Biome zero issues. GitHub.png and the OG/SEO image are NOT touched (DDD-9/DDD-5 exclusions hold). Orchestrator live-verifies the migrated image loads from the CDN URL in the running website after COMMIT.
steps.scenario_name: DISTILL S1 (live render) + S2/S3 (helper unit + normalization)
steps.implementation_notes: RED first: author lighthouseAsset.test.ts (helper absent -> fail), then implement the ~10-LOC helper (GREEN). Base = 'https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/LetPeopleWork/Lighthouse@main/docs/assets/'; strip a single leading '/' from the path before concatenation. Pick metricsoverview as the one migrated image (it maps cleanly per the DESIGN mapping). Website uses Vitest (bun/pnpm) — match the repo's existing test/build scripts (vitest run; build = tsc + vite; Biome via prebuild). Do NOT push the website repo — orchestrator handles push after live verification + user review.

phases.name: One-time refresh of all 10 website screenshots
phases.description: Resolve the mapping audit (OQ-1/OQ-2), produce any missing canonical marketing assets via the existing @screenshot pipeline (run live), then migrate the remaining website references and remove dead bundled copies.

steps.name: Portfolio-metrics marketing @screenshot tests (Lighthouse E2E), run live, regenerate canonical assets
steps.criteria: LIGHTHOUSE REPO. The 10->canonical mapping audit is resolved and recorded (which map directly vs which need a new shot — OQ-1: Query_Configuration/Metrics_Team_2/Forecasts_Team_Epics; OQ-2: the 2 portfolio-metrics gaps). Each confirmed gap has a focused per-theme @screenshot test extending Screenshots.spec.ts (driven from testWithDemoData, written via getPathToDocsAssetsFolder()). New @screenshot tests are RUN LIVE against a clean backend (not just type-checked) and write canonical PNGs into docs/assets/**. If the final gap count exceeds 2, the overflow is flagged for a follow-up bespoke-shot slice (US-02 hypothesis).
steps.implementation_notes: Reuse the existing @screenshot pattern (DDD-10) — no parallel pipeline. Destructure testData from testWithDemoData or the demo fixture won't run. If a small change wouldn't clear the 0.5% pixel-diff threshold, rm the stale target PNG first to force a fresh write, then Read it to confirm. Per-theme (one test per theme), not a monolith.

steps.name: Migrate remaining website screenshots to jsDelivr URLs + migration guard + remove dead imports
steps.criteria: WEBSITE REPO. The remaining still-screenshots (all except GitHub.png) in Lighthouse.tsx and LighthouseSection.tsx are migrated from bundled imports to lighthouseAsset(...) URLs, each mapped to its canonical docs/assets path (from 02-01). A Vitest migration-guard asserts: every migrated mediaItems entry is a jsDelivr URL; GitHub.png stays a bundled import; the OG/SEO image stays same-origin (DDD-5/DDD-9). Dead bundled copies for migrated images are removed from src/assets/screenshots/ with no dangling imports. Website build + Biome clean. Orchestrator live-verifies all Lighthouse images load from the CDN on the running site.
steps.scenario_name: DISTILL S4 (all migrated reference CDN) + S5 (exclusions preserved)
steps.implementation_notes: Use the lighthouseAsset() helper from 01-01. Keep GitHub.png import and the ogImage/structuredData.screenshot same-origin references untouched. Map each website image to the canonical path confirmed in 02-01. Do NOT push — orchestrator handles push after live verification + user review.

phases.name: Finalization website-freshness gate
phases.description: Keep the website fresh by a manual gate at finalization, mirroring the existing per-feature docs/screenshot discipline (D7/DDD-7).

steps.name: Website-freshness finalization gate in CLAUDE.md DELIVER mandate
steps.criteria: LIGHTHOUSE REPO. A Website-freshness gate item is added to the CLAUDE.md 'DELIVER Wave - Docs & Screenshots at Finalization' mandate, mirroring the existing docs/screenshot discipline: changed marketed UI surface -> regenerate canonical docs/assets asset (auto-served to the website via jsDelivr @main) -> confirm the website references the correct asset path. The gate requires an explicit answer (incl. 'N/A, because...'). nw-finalize reads CLAUDE.md, so the reference is via that mandate. Wording matches the existing terse house style. Docs-only change -> DES-exempt.
steps.implementation_notes: Single additive bullet block under the existing DELIVER mandate. DES-exempt (docs) — mark with <!-- DES-ENFORCEMENT : exempt --> in the dispatch if run via crafter, or apply directly as a docs edit.
